reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 185 cfs @ Fort Collins , 3 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir With the recent snowstorm, there’s been a big push of water coming through the canyon. Flows are back up, and big bug season is underway on the Poudre. Dry-dropper rigs continue to be our bread and butter. As water levels rise, larger foam flies in sizes #6–10 are producing well, with hoppers and bigger stoneflies just starting to show up. Ants and beetles are still getting attention too, especially patterns like a #16 Flathead Beetle. As spring settles in on the Poudre, bug diversity continues to improve. Larger stonefly and caddis imitations have been finding success, and the increase in flows has fish looking for bigger meals. Stoneflies in the #6–12 range, along with flashy patterns like the Ticket, have been some of the most productive options lately. Fishing bigger dry-dropper setups has been the key to triggering fish. Spring is definitely starting to show its face around here, and we’re getting more guide trips out on the Poudre every week. Give us a call to get the latest river report and current conditions! Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year.
